source.title: Noradrenergic modulation of rhythmic neural activity shapes selective attention

confidence_rationale: Strong evidence that NE (locus coeruleus) modulates both frequency and amplitude of cortical oscillations; elevated NE shifts thalamic pacemakers from burst-firing (slow oscillations) to tonic single-spike mode (faster, information-transmitting oscillations). Maps onto MECH-093 z_beta/arousal → E3 heartbeat rate modulation. Caveat: NE modulation acts on multiple oscillatory bands and the specific E3 heartbeat-rate-control distinction (vs precision-weighting) is a REE architectural choice not directly evidenced.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Locus coeruleus-norepinephrine (LC-NE) activity modulates the frequency and synchrony of thalamocortical oscillations: elevated NE shifts thalamic neurons from slow burst-firing (low arousal, low oscillation frequency) to faster tonic single-spike mode (high arousal, higher oscillation frequency), enabling more reliable and higher-rate information transmission.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-093: z_beta (the REE affective latent, mapping primarily to LC-NE arousal axis) modulates E3 heartbeat frequency, not only E3 prediction error precision-weighting (MECH-059). High z_beta → elevated NE-equivalent signal → thalamic pacemaker (MD thalamus for E3) shifts toward faster update rate. Low z_beta → slower, energy-efficient E3 update regime. This distinguishes rate-control (MECH-093) from gain-control (MECH-059).
mapping.mapping_caveat: The NE → oscillation frequency pathway operates primarily on thalamocortical alpha/theta bands, not specifically on a deliberative 'E3 heartbeat' band. The mapping requires assuming that E3's deliberation rate corresponds to a thalamocortically-paced oscillation sensitive to LC-NE arousal — a plausible but indirect inference. Also: NE modulates multiple brain systems simultaneously; the specific E3-rate effect is a subset of a broader arousal effect.
mapping.source_context: Review of human EEG, pupillometry, and pharmacological NE manipulation studies; attention and cognitive control tasks.

confidence_components.notes: Good source; NE → oscillation frequency principle is well-supported; REE-specific mapping (E3 heartbeat rate as the target) is architectural inference, not directly testable from this literature alone.
